title: dahlia purpurea var. pallida
intro: Dahlia purpurea var. pallida, also known as the pale dahlia, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and Guatemala. It is a variety of the dahlia purpurea plant, characterized by its pale, almost white flowers. Traditionally, the roots and tubers of the dahlia purpurea var. pallida plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, promoting digestive health and immune system function.
